story_format.i_want: Each step to implement its own agent-config that specifies permissions required for that step's actions
story_format.so_that: The Devin agent can run with minimal necessary permissions in non-interactive mode, improving security and control

target_persona.name: Step Developer
target_persona.permissions: Can define step configurations, read/write step definitions
target_persona.context: Develops individual steps that run via Devin CLI in non-interactive mode. Needs fine-grained control over what permissions each step's agent receives.

capability_breakdown.core_action: Extend step architecture to support per-step agent configuration
outputs_expected: Devin wrapper receives and applies step-specific config
state_changes: Step definition now includes optional agent-config field
Devin wrapper loads step-specific config instead of global config
side_effects: Backward compatibility: steps without agent-config use global config
Improved security: steps only get permissions they need

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Step definition can include optional 'agent_config' field pointing to a config file
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step.json with 'agent_config' field, When the step is executed, Then the specified config is used

acceptance_criteria.criterion: If step has no agent_config, fall back to global .devin/agent-config.json
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step.json without 'agent_config' field, When the step is executed, Then the global agent config is used
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Maintain backward compatibility with existing steps

acceptance_criteria.criterion: DevinWrapper receives agent-config path and passes it to Devin CLI
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step with agent_config, When DevinWrapper builds the Devin command, Then --agent-config flag uses the step-specific config
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Pass step-specific config to Devin agent

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Step-specific agent-config is resolved relative to step directory
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step.json with agent_config: 'agent-config.json', When the path is resolved, Then it points to <step-dir>/agent-config.json

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Validation ensures agent_config file exists if specified
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step.json with non-existent agent_config file, When validation runs, Then an error is raised

raw_request: User request: Extend step architecture so each step can implement its own agent-config, passed to devin wrapper for permission control in non-interactive mode
